name: UUID Generator
description: Generate unique identifiers for your applications

faq.question: What is UUID v4?
faq.answer: UUID v4 is a universally unique identifier generated using random numbers.

faq.question: Is this tool secure?
faq.answer: Yes. UUIDs are generated locally using the crypto module and are not sent to any server.

name: Timestamp Converter
description: Convert between timestamp and human-readable date formats

faq.question: What is a Unix timestamp?
faq.answer: A Unix timestamp is the number of seconds that have elapsed since January 1, 1970 (UTC).

faq.question: Can I convert both ways?
faq.answer: Yes. You can convert both from and to Unix timestamps.

name: What's My IP
description: Quickly check your public IP address and location information

faq.question: What information does this tool show?
faq.answer: This tool displays your public IP address, IP version (IPv4/IPv6), approximate location, and ISP information.

faq.question: Is my privacy protected when using this tool?
faq.answer: Yes. Your IP address is only used to display information to you and is not stored or shared with third parties.

faq.question: Why might I need to know my IP address?
faq.answer: Knowing your IP address can be useful for network troubleshooting, configuring remote connections, or verifying VPN functionality.

name: Password Generator
description: Generate secure, random passwords with customizable options

faq.question: How secure are the generated passwords?
faq.answer: Passwords are generated using cryptographically secure random number generation and are not stored anywhere.

faq.question: Can I customize the password options?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust length, include/exclude uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters.

faq.question: What makes a password strong?
faq.answer: Strong passwords are long (12+ characters), use a mix of character types, and are unique for each account.

name: URL Encoder/Decoder
description: Encode and decode URLs and URL components for web development

faq.question: What is URL encoding?
faq.answer: URL encoding converts characters into a format that can be transmitted over the Internet by replacing unsafe ASCII characters with a '%' followed by two hexadecimal digits.

faq.question: When should I use URL encoding?
faq.answer: Use URL encoding when you need to include special characters in URLs, form data, or query parameters.

faq.question: Is this tool safe to use with sensitive data?
faq.answer: Yes, all encoding and decoding is performed locally in your browser. No data is sent to any server.

name: Base64 Encoder/Decoder
description: Encode and decode Base64 strings for data transmission and storage

faq.question: What is Base64 encoding?
faq.answer: Base64 is a binary-to-text encoding scheme that represents binary data in ASCII characters using a 64-character set (A-Z, a-z, 0-9, +, /).

faq.question: When should I use Base64 encoding?
faq.answer: Base64 is commonly used for encoding binary data in emails, embedding images in HTML/CSS, API tokens, and data URLs.

faq.question: Is this tool secure for sensitive data?
faq.answer: Yes, all encoding and decoding happens locally in your browser. However, Base64 is encoding, not encryption - don't use it for securing sensitive data.
